heading: Reserves for losses on loans of banks
label: 585 Reserves for losses on loans of banks
notes: Amendments1996—Subsec. (a)(2)(A). struck out “other than an organization to which section 593 applies” after “section 581)”.Pub. L. 104–1881990—Subsec. (b)(1). , substituted “shall not exceed the addition to the reserve for losses on loans determined under the experience method as provided in paragraph (2).” for “shall not exceed the greater of—Pub. L. 101–508, § 11801(c)(12)(C)“(A) for taxable years beginning before 1988 the addition to the reserve for losses on loans determined under the percentage method as provided in paragraph (2), or“(B) the addition to the reserve for losses on loans determined under the experience method as provided in paragraph (3).”Subsec. (b)(2). , (c)(12)(D), redesignated par. (3) as (2) and struck out former par. (2) which related to use of percentage method for determining amount to add to reserve for bad debts.Pub. L. 101–508, § 11801(a)(26)Subsec. (b)(3). , (E), redesignated par. (4) as (3), substituted heading for one which read: “Regulations; definition of eligible loan, etc.”, and amended text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “The Secretary shall define the terms ‘loan’ and ‘eligible loan’ and prescribe such regulations as may be necessary to carry out the purposes of this section; except that the term ‘eligible loan’ shall not include—Pub. L. 101–508, § 11801(c)(12)(D)“(A) a loan to a bank (as defined in section 581),“(B) a loan to a domestic branch of a foreign corporation to which subsection (a)(2) applies,“(C) a loan secured by a deposit (i) in the lending bank, or (ii) in an institution described in subparagraph (A) or (B) if the lending bank has control over withdrawal of such deposit,“(D) a loan to or guaranteed by the United States, a possession or instrumentality thereof, or a State or a political subdivision thereof,“(E) a loan evidenced by a security as defined in section 165(g)(2)(C),“(F) a loan of Federal funds, and“(G) commercial paper, including short-term promissory notes which may be purchased on the open market.” Former par. (3) redesignated (2).Subsec. (b)(4). , redesignated par. (4) as (3).Pub. L. 101–508, § 11801(c)(12)(D)1988—Subsec. (c)(3)(B)(ii). , substituted “elects a higher percentage” for “designates an amount”.Pub. L. 100–647, § 1009(a)(2)(C)Subsec. (c)(4). , inserted at end “If the amount of the reserve referred to in subparagraph (B) as of the close of any taxable year exceeds the outstanding balance (as of such time) of the loans referred to in subparagraph (B), such excess shall be included in gross income for such taxable year.”Pub. L. 100–647, § 1009(a)(3)Subsec. (c)(5)(C). , added subpar. (C).Pub. L. 100–647, § 1009(a)(2)(A)1987—Subsec. (c)(3)(C). substituted “section 6655(e)(2)(A)(i)” for “section 6655(d)(3)”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 901(a)(1)“(1) any bank (as defined in section 581) other than an organization to which section 593 applies, and“(2) any corporation to which paragraph (1) would apply except for the fact that it is a foreign corporation, and in the case of any such foreign corporation this section shall apply only with respect to loans outstanding the interest on which is effectively connected with the conduct of a banking business within the United States.”Subsec. (b)(1). , substituted “subsection (a)” for “section 166(c)”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 901(d)(1)Subsec. (c). , added subsec. (c).Pub. L. 99–514, § 901(a)(2)1981—Subsec. children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (c), a bank shall be allowed a deduction for a reasonable addition to a reserve for bad debts. Such deduction shall be in lieu of any deduction under section 166(a).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “bank” means any bank (as defined in section 581).

children.heading: Banking business of United States branch of foreign corporation
children.label: B Banking business of United States branch of foreign corporation
children.text: The term “bank” also includes any corporation to which subparagraph (A) would apply except for the fact that it is a foreign corporation. In the case of any such foreign corporation, this section shall apply only with respect to loans outstanding the interest on which is effectively connected with the conduct of a banking business within the United States.

children.heading: Addition to reserves for bad debts
children.label: b Addition to reserves for bad debts

children.heading: General rule
children.label: 1 General rule
children.text: For purposes of subsection (a), the reasonable addition to the reserve for bad debts of any financial institution to which this section applies shall be an amount determined by the taxpayer which shall not exceed the addition to the reserve for losses on loans determined under the experience method as provided in paragraph (2).

children.label: A
children.text: the amount which bears the same ratio to loans outstanding at the close of the taxable year as (i) the total bad debts sustained during the taxable year and the 5 preceding taxable years (or, with the approval of the Secretary, a shorter period), adjusted for recoveries of bad debts during such period, bears to (ii) the sum of the loans outstanding at the close of such 6 or fewer taxable years, or

children.label: i
children.text: the balance of the reserve at the close of the base year, or

children.label: ii
children.text: if the amount of loans outstanding at the close of the taxable year is less than the amount of loans outstanding at the close of the base year, the amount which bears the same ratio to loans outstanding at the close of the taxable year as the balance of the reserve at the close of the base year bears to the amount of loans outstanding at the close of the base year.

children.heading: Regulations; definition of loan
children.label: 3 Regulations; definition of loan
children.text: The Secretary shall define the term loan and prescribe such regulations as may be necessary to carry out the purposes of this section.

children.heading: Section not to apply to large banks
children.label: c Section not to apply to large banks

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In the case of a large bank, this section shall not apply (and no deduction shall be allowed under any other provision of this subtitle for any addition to a reserve for bad debts).

children.label: A
children.text: the average adjusted bases of all assets of such bank exceeded $500,000,000, or

children.label: B
children.text: such bank was a member of a parent-subsidiary controlled group and the average adjusted bases of all assets of such group exceeded $500,000,000.

children.label: i
children.text: the provisions of this subsection shall be treated as a change in the method of accounting of such bank for the disqualification year,

children.label: ii
children.text: such change shall be treated as having been made with the consent of the Secretary, and

children.label: I
children.text: the amount taken into account for the 1st of such taxable years being the greater of 10 percent of such net amount or such higher percentage of such net amount as the taxpayer may elect, and

children.label: II
children.text: the amount taken into account in each of the 3 succeeding taxable years being equal to the applicable fraction (determined in accordance with the following table for the taxable year involved) of the portion of such net amount not taken into account under subclause (I).The applicableIf the case of the—fraction is—1st succeeding year⁄292nd succeeding year⅓3rd succeeding year⁄.49

children.heading: Suspension of recapture for taxable year for which bank is financially troubled
children.label: B Suspension of recapture for taxable year for which bank is financially troubled
I
children.text: no adjustment shall be taken into account under subparagraph (A) for such taxable year, and

children.label: II
children.text: such taxable year shall be disregarded in determining whether any other taxable year is a taxable year for which an adjustment is required to be taken into account under subparagraph (A) or the amount of such adjustment.

children.heading: Exception for elective recapture for 1st year
children.label: ii Exception for elective recapture for 1st year
children.text: Clause (i) shall not apply to the 1st taxable year referred to in subparagraph (A)(iii)(I) if the taxpayer elects a higher percentage in accordance with such subparagraph.

children.heading: Financially troubled bank
children.label: iii Financially troubled bank
children.text: For purposes of clause (i), the term “financially troubled bank” means any bank if, for the taxable year, the nonperforming loan percentage of such bank exceeds 75 percent.

children.label: I
children.text: the sum of the outstanding balances of nonperforming loans of the bank as of the close of each quarter of the taxable year, by

children.label: II
children.text: the sum of the amounts of equity of the bank as of the close of each such quarter.

children.heading: Nonperforming loans
children.label: I Nonperforming loans
children.text: The term “nonperforming loan” means any loan which is considered to be nonperforming by the primary Federal regulatory agency with respect to the bank.

children.heading: Equity
children.label: II Equity
children.text: The term “equity” means the equity of the bank as determined for Federal regulatory purposes.

children.heading: Coordination with estimated tax payments
children.label: C Coordination with estimated tax payments
children.text: For purposes of applying section 6655(e)(2)(A)(i) with respect to any installment, the determination under subparagraph (B) of whether an adjustment is required to be taken into account under subparagraph (A) shall be made as of the last day prescribed for payment of such installment.

children.label: A
children.text: the provisions of this subsection shall not be treated as a change in the method of accounting of the taxpayer for purposes of section 481,

children.label: B
children.text: the taxpayer shall continue to maintain its reserve for loans held by the bank as of the 1st day of the disqualification year and charge against such reserve any losses resulting from loans held by the bank as of such 1st day, and

children.label: C
children.text: no deduction shall be allowed under this section (or any other provision of this subtitle) for any addition to such reserve for the disqualification year or any subsequent taxable year.

children.heading: Parent-subsidiary controlled group
children.label: A Parent-subsidiary controlled group
children.text: The term “parent-subsidiary controlled group” means any controlled group of corporations described in section 1563(a)(1). In determining the average adjusted bases of assets held by such a group, interests held by one member of such group in another member of such group shall be disregarded.

children.heading: Disqualification year
children.label: B Disqualification year
children.text: The term “disqualification year” means, with respect to any bank, the 1st taxable year beginning after , for which such bank was a large bank if such bank maintained a reserve for bad debts for the preceding taxable year.December 31, 1986

children.heading: Election made by each member
children.label: C Election made by each member
children.text: In the case of a parent-subsidiary controlled group, any election under this section shall be made separately by each member of such group.
